title: Memory with write masks results in array of memories of mask width

features.name: memory with byte-enable write mask
features.description: Code should include a memory declaration that uses a byte-enable mask for partial word writes, where each bit of the mask controls writing to a corresponding byte lane

features.name: unpacked array representing masked memory
features.description: Code should include a memory modeled as an unpacked array of smaller chunks (e.g., byte array representing a larger word width) which should instead be a single memory with mask ports
features.snippet:
reg [7:0] mem [0:1023]; // 8-bit wide memory cells
wire [7:0] read_data = mem[read_addr];

features.name: syncreadmem with vector element type
features.description: Code should include a synchronous read memory declaration with vector or array element type intended for byte-enable support

features.name: conditional write to memory byte lane
features.description: Code should include conditional writes to individual byte lanes of a memory word using a byte mask signal
features.snippet:
for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
  if (wmask[i]) mem[addr][i*8+:8] <= wdata[i*8+:8];
